Both are likely off-by-one errors in the lipgloss layout height calculations. Key files: internal/tui/layout.go CalculatePaneDimensions (lines 36-104), internal/tui/model.go renderNewLayout (lines 743-880). The bottom bars (TextEntry + StatusBar) are joined after the main pane layout - check height accounting there.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"michael.biancalana@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-22T23:54:18Z","created_by":"Mike Biancalana","updated_at":"2026-03-22T23:54:18Z"}

{"id":"reckon-0i4v","title":"Spike: tmux integration for agent TUI visual assessment","description":"Explore using tmux to allow AI agents to visually inspect the TUI with ANSI escape codes rendered. The goal is to enable agents to see the actual rendered terminal UI (colors, borders, layout) rather than raw text output. Research: launching TUI inside a tmux pane, capturing rendered output via 'tmux capture-pane -e', feeding screenshot to agent vision. This would dramatically improve agent ability to verify TUI correctness.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"michael.biancalana@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-22T23:54:13Z","created_by":"Mike Biancalana","updated_at":"2026-03-22T23:54:13Z"}

new_value:
{"notes":"Root cause identified. In renderNewLayout() (model.go:870):

    mainView := content + "\n" + textEntry + "\n" + successMsg + "\n" + summary + "\n" + status

When successMsg is empty string (the common case), the join produces:
    content + textEntry + "" + summary + status with 4 explicit newlines

Height breakdown for a 50-line terminal:
- availableHeight = 50 - 5 = 45 (correctly subtracts TextEntryHeight(3) + SummaryHeight(1) + StatusHeight(1))
- content: 45 lines
- textEntry: 3 lines
- empty successMsg: still counts as 1 blank line due to surrounding "\n"s
- summary: 1 line
- status: 1 line
Total: 51 lines rendered into a 50-line terminal → top line clipped

The 'spurious line below text entry' is the blank line created by "\n" + "" + "\n" when successMsg is empty.

Fix: conditionally include successMsg/summary in the join only when non-empty:
    parts := []string{content, textEntry}
    if successMsg != "" { parts = append(parts, successMsg) }
    if summary != "" { parts = append(parts, summary) }
    parts = append(parts, status)
    mainView = strings.Join(parts, "\n")

Confirmed via tmux capture-pane + ansi2html + playwright screenshot: top border (┌──┐) of all three panes is clipped off screen."}

new_value:
{"notes":"Spike complete. Workflow validated:
1. Build binary: go build -o /tmp/rk-test ./cmd/rk/
2. Launch in tmux: /usr/bin/tmux new-session -d -s reckon-test -x 220 -y 50 '/tmp/rk-test'
3. Capture with ANSI: /usr/bin/tmux capture-pane -t reckon-test -p -e > capture.txt
4. Convert to HTML: .venv/bin/ansi2html < capture.txt > render.html (use uv venv + ansi2html)
5. Serve + screenshot: python3 -m http.server 8877 & then playwright browser_navigate + browser_take_screenshot

Result: agents can clearly see rendered TUI with borders, colors, and layout. The screenshot was immediately diagnostic for the layout bug (top border visibly missing).

Key detail: tmux -V = 3.6a, binary at /usr/bin/tmux (zsh alias shadows it). Python packages via uv venv in project root."}

{"description":"## Problem
Open tasks that haven't been touched in a long time accumulate silently. There's no way to review them or decide to close/defer/reprioritize without manually scanning the full task list.

## Proposed Command
\`\`\`
rk task stale              # list open tasks created >30 days ago, no recent notes
rk task stale --days=14    # configurable threshold
rk task stale --interactive  # prompt: keep / defer / close for each
\`\`\`

## Staleness Definition
A task is stale when:
- Status is open (not done)
- CreatedAt is older than threshold (default: 30 days)
- No task notes have been added since the threshold window

## Output Format (non-interactive)
One line per task:
\`\`\`
abc123  (47 days)  Refactor auth middleware  [backend, api]
xyz789  (61 days)  Write migration guide
\`\`\`

## Interactive Mode (--interactive)
For each stale task, show it and prompt:
- k  keep (no change, skip)
- d  defer (prompts for date, sets scheduled_date)
- c  close (marks done)
- q  quit

## Implementation Notes
- Query tasks WHERE status='open' AND created_at < (now - threshold)
- Filter out tasks that have task notes added recently (note ordering by position is a proxy)
- Lives in internal/cli/task.go as a new subcommand under rk task
- No TUI component needed for initial version — pure CLI output

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] rk task stale lists open tasks older than 30 days
- [ ] --days flag adjusts threshold
- [ ] Output includes age in days, task text, and tags
- [ ] --interactive mode prompts keep/defer/close for each","title":"Add rk task stale command to surface long-untouched open tasks"}

{"id":"reckon-pfpb","title":"No way to list or search zettelkasten notes","description":"## Problem
There is currently no way to list or search zettelkasten notes from the CLI. The `rk notes` command only supports `create`, `edit`, and `show` subcommands, requiring users to know the exact slug of a note to view it.

## Solution
Implement `rk notes search` subcommand with fuzzy search and filtering capabilities.

## Behavior
- **With no arguments**: Launch a small TUI with fuzzy search interface (similar to how `rk notes edit` launches a picker)
- **With search term**: Perform CLI-based search and output results to stdout
- **Filtering**: Support filtering by tags (e.g., `--tag=foo` or `--tags=foo,bar`)

## Implementation Details

### TUI Mode (no arguments)
- Use bubbletea to create a fuzzy search interface
- Display notes with: title, slug, tags, creation date
- Allow filtering by tags with inline syntax or keybindings
- Select a note to view its details (show content, links, backlinks)
- ESC to cancel, Enter to select

### CLI Mode (with arguments)
```bash
rk notes search "keyword"              # Search titles and content
rk notes search "keyword" --tag=foo    # Search with tag filter
rk notes search --tag=foo,bar          # Show all notes with tags
```

### Search Implementation
- Search across: note titles, slugs, content, and tags
- Use fuzzy matching (e.g., go-fuzzyfinder or similar)
- Rank results by relevance
- Output format: one line per note with slug, title, and tags

### Files to Modify/Create
- `internal/cli/notes.go` - Add `notesSearchCmd`
- Create new search service in `internal/service/` if needed
- Use existing `NotesService.GetAllNotes()` as data source
- May need to add `NotesService.SearchNotes(query, tags)` method

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `rk notes search` with no args launches TUI fuzzy search
- [ ] `rk notes search "term"` searches and outputs matching notes to CLI
- [ ] `--tag` flag filters results by tags
- [ ] TUI interface allows selecting a note to view details
- [ ] Search is reasonably fast even with hundreds of notes
- [ ] Help text documents the command and its options","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"michael.biancalana@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:00:37Z","created_by":"Mike Biancalana","updated_at":"2026-02-15T16:37:04Z","work_type":"mutex"}

{"id":"reckon-gzr","title":"Implement full-text search for notes","description":"## Overview
Implement full-text search across all notes, enabling users to quickly find notes by content, title, or tags. Use SQLite FTS5 (Full-Text Search) for efficient searching with support for ranking, highlighting, and partial matches.

## Background Context
- Notes are stored as markdown files with content in `~/.reckon/notes/`
- SQLite FTS5 provides fast full-text search with advanced features
- Must search across note titles, content (excluding frontmatter), and tags
- Results should be ranked by relevance
- Must integrate with existing notes schema from reckon-63n

## Acceptance Criteria

### 1. Command Interface

**Basic usage**:
```bash
rk note search "zettelkasten method"
```

**With options**:
```bash
rk note search "atomic notes" --limit 10
rk note search "learning" --tag productivity
rk note search "database" --format json
```

**Flags**:
- `--limit` / `-n` - Max number of results (default: 20)
- `--tag` / `-t` - Filter by tag
- `--title-only` - Search titles only
- `--format` - Output format: `text`, `json`, `table` (default: text)
- `--context` / `-C` - Show N lines of context around matches

**Command structure**:
```
rk note search QUERY [flags]
```

- [ ] Accept search query as positional argument
- [ ] Support `--limit` to control result count
- [ ] Support `--tag` to filter by tag
- [ ] Support `--title-only` for title-only search
- [ ] Support `--format` for different output formats
- [ ] Support `--context` to show surrounding lines

### 2. FTS5 Virtual Table Setup

Create FTS5 virtual table for indexing note content:

**Migration**: `internal/db/migrations/XXXXXX_create_notes_fts.sql`

```sql
-- Create FTS5 virtual table for notes
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notes_fts USING fts5(
    slug UNINDEXED,        -- Note slug (not searchable, for joining)
    title,                  -- Searchable note title
    content,                -- Searchable note content (without frontmatter)
    tags,                   -- Searchable tags (space-separated)
    content=notes,          -- Content comes from 'notes' table
    content_rowid=id        -- Join on notes.id
);

-- Triggers to keep FTS5 in sync with notes table

-- Trigger: After note insert
CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS notes_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON notes BEGIN
    INSERT INTO notes_fts(rowid, slug, title, content, tags)
    VALUES (new.id, new.slug, new.title, '', new.tags);
    -- Note: content filled separately by application after file parse
END;

-- Trigger: After note update
CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS notes_fts_update AFTER UPDATE ON notes BEGIN
    UPDATE notes_fts 
    SET slug = new.slug,
        title = new.title,
        tags = new.tags
    WHERE rowid = old.id;
END;

-- Trigger: After note delete
CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS notes_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON notes BEGIN
    DELETE FROM notes_fts WHERE rowid = old.id;
END;
```

- [ ] Create FTS5 virtual table with title, content, tags columns
- [ ] Set up triggers to keep FTS5 in sync with notes table
- [ ] Test migration creates table and triggers correctly

### 3. Indexing Note Content

When a note is created or updated, index its content:

**Service layer** (`internal/service/note_service.go`):

```go
// IndexNoteContent extracts markdown content and indexes in FTS5
func (s *NoteService) IndexNoteContent(noteID uint, filePath string) error {
    // Read and parse markdown file
    content, err := s.readNoteContent(filePath)
    if err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("failed to read note content: %w", err)
    }
    
    // Remove frontmatter (don't index it)
    content = removeFrontmatter(content)
    
    // Update FTS5 table
    return s.db.Exec(`
        UPDATE notes_fts 
        SET content = ? 
        WHERE rowid = ?
    `, content, noteID).Error
}

func removeFrontmatter(content string) string {
    // Remove YAML frontmatter delimited by ---
    re := regexp.MustCompile(`(?s)^---\n.*?\n---\n`)
    return re.ReplaceAllString(content, "")
}
```

**Integration**:
- Call `IndexNoteContent` after note created/updated
- Update during `rk note new` and note edit operations
- Batch re-index command: `rk note reindex` (optional)

- [ ] Implement `IndexNoteContent` function
- [ ] Remove frontmatter before indexing
- [ ] Call after note creation and updates
- [ ] Handle file read errors gracefully

### 4. Search Query Implementation

**Service layer** (`internal/service/note_service.go`):

```go
type SearchResult struct {
    Note      models.Note
    Rank      float64     // FTS5 rank score
    Snippet   string      // Highlighted snippet of match
    MatchType string      // "title", "content", "tags"
}

// SearchNotes performs full-text search across notes
func (s *NoteService) SearchNotes(query string, options SearchOptions) ([]SearchResult, error) {
    // Build FTS5 query
    ftsQuery := query
    if options.TitleOnly {
        ftsQuery = "title:" + query
    }
    
    // Build SQL query
    sql := `
        SELECT 
            notes.*,
            rank,
            snippet(notes_fts, 1, '<mark>', '</mark>', '...', 32) as snippet
        FROM notes_fts
        JOIN notes ON notes.id = notes_fts.rowid
        WHERE notes_fts MATCH ?
        ORDER BY rank
        LIMIT ?
    `
    
    var results []SearchResult
    rows, err := s.db.Raw(sql, ftsQuery, options.Limit).Rows()
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    defer rows.Close()
    
    for rows.Next() {
        var result SearchResult
        // Scan into result
        results = append(results, result)
    }
    
    return results, nil
}

type SearchOptions struct {
    Limit      int
    Tag        string
    TitleOnly  bool
    Format     string
    Context    int
}
```

**FTS5 query syntax**:
- Simple: `"zettelkasten method"` - exact phrase
- AND: `zettelkasten AND method` - both terms
- OR: `zettelkasten OR slip-box` - either term
- Column: `title:atomic` - search in title only
- Prefix: `learn*` - prefix matching

- [ ] Implement `SearchNotes` function with FTS5 query
- [ ] Use `rank` for result ordering
- [ ] Use `snippet()` for highlighted excerpts
- [ ] Support FTS5 query syntax (AND, OR, phrases, prefix)
- [ ] Apply tag filter if specified

### 5. Result Formatting

**Text format** (default):
```
3 results for "zettelkasten method"

1. atomic-notes (rank: 12.5)
   Atomic Notes in the Zettelkasten Method
   ...uses the <mark>zettelkasten</mark> <mark>method</mark> to organize knowledge into...
   
2. slip-box (rank: 8.3)
   The Slip-Box Technique
   ...original German <mark>zettelkasten</mark> <mark>method</mark> developed by Niklas...

3. permanent-notes (rank: 6.1)
   Understanding Permanent Notes
   ...key concept in the <mark>zettelkasten</mark> system...
```

**JSON format** (`--format json`):
```json
{
  "query": "zettelkasten method",
  "count": 3,
  "results": [
    {
      "slug": "atomic-notes",
      "title": "Atomic Notes in the Zettelkasten Method",
      "rank": 12.5,
      "snippet": "...uses the zettelkasten method to organize...",
      "file_path": "atomic-notes.md",
      "tags": ["productivity", "learning"]
    }
  ]
}
```

**Table format** (`--format table`):
```
┌──────────────┬──────────────────────────────┬──────┬────────────────────┐
│ SLUG         │ TITLE                        │ RANK │ TAGS               │
├──────────────┼──────────────────────────────┼──────┼────────────────────┤
│ atomic-notes │ Atomic Notes in Zettelkasten │ 12.5 │ productivity       │
│ slip-box     │ The Slip-Box Technique       │ 8.3  │ learning, notes    │
│ permanent... │ Understanding Permanent...   │ 6.1  │ zettelkasten       │
└──────────────┴──────────────────────────────┴──────┴────────────────────┘
```

- [ ] Implement text output with rank and snippet
- [ ] Implement JSON output with all fields
- [ ] Implement table output with key fields
- [ ] Show match count in output
- [ ] Highlight search terms in snippet (use `<mark>` tags)

### 6. Tag Filtering

When `--tag` specified, filter results:

```go
func (s *NoteService) SearchNotes(query string, options SearchOptions) ([]SearchResult, error) {
    sql := `...`
    args := []interface{}{ftsQuery, options.Limit}
    
    // Add tag filter
    if options.Tag != "" {
        sql += ` AND notes.tags LIKE ?`
        args = append(args, "%\""+options.Tag+"\"%")
    }
    
    // Execute query
}
```

- [ ] Filter results by tag when `--tag` specified
- [ ] Use JSON array LIKE matching for tag filter
- [ ] Combine FTS5 search with tag filter efficiently

### 7. Context Lines

Show surrounding lines when `--context` specified:

```go
func addContext(content string, match string, contextLines int) string {
    lines := strings.Split(content, "\n")
    matchIndex := -1
    
    // Find line with match
    for i, line := range lines {
        if strings.Contains(line, match) {
            matchIndex = i
            break
        }
    }
    
    if matchIndex == -1 {
        return match
    }
    
    // Extract context window
    start := max(0, matchIndex-contextLines)
    end := min(len(lines), matchIndex+contextLines+1)
    
    contextLines := lines[start:end]
    return strings.Join(contextLines, "\n")
}
```

- [ ] Extract N lines before and after match
- [ ] Show line numbers for context
- [ ] Handle multiple matches in same note

### 8. Search Performance

**Indexing**:
- Index notes in background after creation/update
- Don't block user operations
- Batch re-index for existing notes: `rk note reindex`

**Querying**:
- FTS5 is very fast (sub-millisecond for thousands of notes)
- Use `rank` for relevance ordering
- LIMIT results to avoid overwhelming output

**Optimization**:
- Create indexes on notes table for tag filtering
- Use covering index for common queries
- Consider incremental indexing for large note collections

- [ ] Optimize FTS5 queries for performance
- [ ] Add indexes for tag filtering
- [ ] Test with 1000+ notes to verify performance

## Implementation Guidance

### File Structure
```
internal/
  db/
    migrations/
      XXXXXX_create_notes_fts.sql  # NEW: FTS5 table and triggers
  service/
    note_service.go                # MODIFY: Add SearchNotes, IndexNoteContent
cmd/
  note/
    search.go                       # NEW: 'rk note search' command
```

### Cobra Command

**File**: `cmd/note/search.go`

```go
var searchCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "search QUERY",
    Short: "Search notes by content",
    Long: `Full-text search across all note titles, content, and tags.
    
Supports FTS5 query syntax:
  - Exact phrase: "zettelkasten method"
  - AND: zettelkasten AND method
  - OR: zettelkasten OR slip-box
  - Column search: title:atomic
  - Prefix: learn*
`,
    Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
    RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
        query := args[0]
        
        options := SearchOptions{
            Limit:     cmd.Flags().GetInt("limit"),
            Tag:       cmd.Flags().GetString("tag"),
            TitleOnly: cmd.Flags().GetBool("title-only"),
            Format:    cmd.Flags().GetString("format"),
            Context:   cmd.Flags().GetInt("context"),
        }
        
        results, err := noteService.SearchNotes(query, options)
        if err != nil {
            return err
        }
        
        // Format and print results
        printSearchResults(results, options.Format)
        return nil
    },
}

func init() {
    searchCmd.Flags().IntP("limit", "n", 20, "Max results")
    searchCmd.Flags().StringP("tag", "t", "", "Filter by tag")
    searchCmd.Flags().Bool("title-only", false, "Search titles only")
    searchCmd.Flags().StringP("format", "f", "text", "Output format (text|json|table)")
    searchCmd.Flags().IntP("context", "C", 0, "Lines of context")
}
```

### Testing Requirements

**Unit tests**:
- Test FTS5 query building
- Test snippet extraction
- Test tag filtering
- Test result formatting (text, JSON, table)
- Test frontmatter removal

**Integration tests**:
- Create sample notes
- Index them in FTS5
- Run searches and verify results
- Test ranking (more relevant results first)
- Test tag filtering
- Test with special characters in query

- [ ] Unit tests for search logic
- [ ] Integration tests with sample notes
- [ ] Test FTS5 indexing and syncing
- [ ] Test all output formats
- [ ] Test edge cases (empty results, special chars)

### Edge Cases

1. **No results**: Show "No notes found for query"
2. **Empty query**: Show error, require query
3. **Special chars in query**: Escape properly for FTS5
4. **Very long results**: Truncate snippet to reasonable length
5. **Notes without content**: Index title and tags only
6. **Deleted notes**: Triggers handle cleanup automatically
7. **Case sensitivity**: FTS5 is case-insensitive by default
8. **Partial matches**: Use prefix operator `learn*`
9. **Tag with special chars**: Escape for LIKE query

## Reindex Command (Optional)

Add command to rebuild FTS5 index:

```bash
rk note reindex [--force]
```

**Implementation**:
```go
func (s *NoteService) ReindexAll(force bool) error {
    // Clear FTS5 table
    s.db.Exec("DELETE FROM notes_fts")
    
    // Fetch all notes
    var notes []models.Note
    s.db.Find(&notes)
    
    // Reindex each note
    for _, note := range notes {
        // Index content from file
        s.IndexNoteContent(note.ID, note.FilePath)
    }
    
    return nil
}
```

- [ ] Implement `rk note reindex` command (optional)
- [ ] Show progress for large note collections
- [ ] Handle errors gracefully (skip corrupted notes)

## Example Usage

**Simple search**:
```bash
$ rk note search "atomic notes"
2 results for "atomic notes"

1. atomic-notes (rank: 15.2)
   Atomic Notes in Zettelkasten
   ...the concept of <mark>atomic</mark> <mark>notes</mark> is fundamental...

2. zettelkasten-method (rank: 8.1)
   Zettelkasten Method Overview
   ...uses <mark>atomic</mark> <mark>notes</mark> to build knowledge...
```

**Search with tag filter**:
```bash
$ rk note search "learning" --tag productivity
1 result for "learning" (tag: productivity)

1. learning-techniques (rank: 12.3)
   Effective Learning Techniques
   ...active <mark>learning</mark> strategies include...
```

**JSON output**:
```bash
$ rk note search "database" --format json | jq '.results[].title'
"SQLite Database Patterns"
"Database Normalization"
"NoSQL vs SQL Databases"
```

- [ ] `--title-only` searches titles only
- [ ] `--format` supports text, json, table outputs
- [ ] `--context` shows surrounding lines
- [ ] Note content indexed automatically on create/update
- [ ] Triggers keep FTS5 in sync with notes table
- [ ] Unit tests >85% coverage
- [ ] Integration tests with sample data
- [ ] Performance tested with 1000+ notes
- [ ] FTS5 query syntax documented in --help

3-4 hours (includes FTS5 setup, indexing, search implementation, testing)

{"id":"reckon-obed","title":"Simplify TUI task list: replace list.Model with []Task + selectedIndex","description":"## Problem

The TUI task list has a ghost-selection bug: completed tasks can be selected even though they're not displayed. This is caused by a fundamental architecture issue — the `TaskList` component wraps Bubble Tea's `list.Model` for cursor tracking, but the rendering path (`renderTasksWithDetailPane`) never calls `TaskList.View()`. Instead it gets tasks via `GetTasks()`, groups them through `GroupTasksByTime()`, and renders via `renderTaskSection()`. The cursor and the display are two parallel views of the same data that get out of sync.

This bug has been worked on multiple times without being fixed because the root cause is architectural, not a missing filter.

## Solution

Replace the `TaskList` component entirely with two plain fields on `Model`:
- `tasks []journal.Task` — open tasks sorted by priority
- `selectedIndex int` — cursor position into the displayed list

Single source of truth. No hidden cursor state. No sync issues.

## Design Decisions

**Priority sort replaces time-grouped sections.** Instead of TODAY / THIS WEEK / ALL TASKS sections with boundary-crossing navigation, a single sorted list: overdue → due today/scheduled today → due this week/scheduled this week → everything else by creation date. Same urgency signal, zero section machinery.

**Notes move to a detail area.** Instead of interleaving note items in the cursor list (which required skip-note navigation, collapse state, `IsSelectedItemNote`), show notes for the selected task in a fixed area below the list.

**Keep:** add task, edit task, toggle done (archive), add note, tags, schedule/deadline dates, date picker modal, date urgency indicators.

**Remove:** `TaskList` component, `TimeGroupedTaskList`, `list.Model`/`TaskItem`/`TaskDelegate`/`buildTaskItems`, collapsible notes in task list, clear date submenu (`c`), delete task from TUI (`d`), detail pane positioning, notes pane stub, `SectionNotes`.

## Implementation Steps

Each step leaves `go build` and `go test` passing.

### Step 1 — Add `SortTasksByPriority` with unit tests
Add to `model.go`. Bucket 0: overdue deadline. Bucket 1: due today or scheduled today/past. Bucket 2: due/scheduled this week. Bucket 3: everything else. Filter out done tasks. Stable sort by CreatedAt within buckets. Table-driven tests.

### Step 2 — Add `tasks`/`selectedIndex` to Model alongside `taskList`
Populate in `handleTasksLoaded` after existing `taskList.UpdateTasks`. Both paths coexist temporarily.

### Step 3 — Implement new render methods (unused)
Add `renderTaskList(width, height)` — simple loop over `m.tasks`, highlight selected, scroll offset. Add `renderDetailArea(width, height)` — shows notes for selected task. Dead code until Step 4.

### Step 4 — Swap rendering, remove section layout infrastructure
Replace `renderTasksWithDetailPane` call in `renderNewLayout` with new methods. Delete: `renderTasksWithDetailPane`, `renderDetailPane`, `renderTaskSection`, `DetailPanePosition`, `getTaskSection`, `calculateDetailPanePosition`, `TaskSectionDimensions`, `CalculateTaskSectionDimensions`, `notesPaneVisible`, notes pane from model. Delete `model_selection_test.go`.

### Step 5 — Wire keyboard navigation to `selectedIndex`
j/k = `clampIndex(selectedIndex±1)`. Space reads `m.tasks[m.selectedIndex]`. Update `handleAddNote`, `handleEditTask`, `handleScheduleTask`, `handleSetDeadline`. Remove: `handleDeleteTask`, `handleClearDate`, `handleClearDateKeys`, `clearDateMode`, `c`/`d` keybindings for tasks, enter-to-expand for tasks, `TaskSelectionChangedMsg`, `handleTaskSelectionChanged`, `updateNotesForSelectedTask`, `updateLinksForSelectedItem`, `selectedTaskID`.

### Step 6 — Delete `TaskList` component and `list.Model` infrastructure
Delete from `task_list.go`: `TaskList`, `TimeGroupedTaskList`, `TaskItem`, `TaskDelegate`, `buildTaskItems`, `GroupTasksByTime`, etc. Keep: `parseDate`, `formatDateInfo`, `formatFriendlyDate`, `getDateStyle`, style vars, `TaskToggleMsg`. Remove `taskList` field from Model. Delete `task_list_example_test.go`. Rewrite `task_list_test.go` for surviving helpers.

### Step 7 — Remove `SectionNotes` and notes pane plumbing
`SectionCount` → 2. Remove `N` keybinding, `handleToggleNotesPane`, `handleLinkSelected`, `handleLinksLoaded`, `linksLoadedMsg`, `loadLinksForNote`. Update help view.

### Step 8 — Rewrite model tests
Tests for: `SortTasksByPriority`, `renderTaskList` empty/selection/scroll, `renderDetailArea` empty/with-notes, help view updated shortcuts.

### Step 9 — Final cleanup
Remove collapse indicator constants if unreferenced. Dead-code grep sweep. `go vet`.

## Risks

- `formatDateInfo`/`getDateStyle` may not be exported — export in Step 3
- Step 4 is the largest change — can split into 4a (swap render) and 4b (delete old code)
- `TaskNoteDeleteMsg` exists in both `model.go` and `components` package — remove both in Step 6
- Scroll offset logic needs explicit test coverage (Step 8)

## Out of Scope

- Task note deletion from detail area (follow-on)
- Scroll indicator / scroll bar
- Task filtering or search
- Notes/wiki pane (separate initiative)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"michael.biancalana@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-28T18:30:56Z","created_by":"Mike Biancalana","updated_at":"2026-03-28T18:30:56Z"}

Tasks sorted by urgency within each section.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Mike Biancalana","owner":"michael.biancalana@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-31T20:51:05Z","created_by":"Opencode","updated_at":"2026-03-29T20:07:54Z","work_type":"mutex"}

new_value:
{"description":"The checklist CLI commands are functional but high-friction for human use. Checking off items requires typing 'rk cl check <pos> --template=<name>' repeatedly. There is also no way to abandon a checklist run without immediately starting a new one (reset = abandon + start).

## Problem
- Checking items requires long CLI commands with --template flag each time
- No way to simply abandon a run (reset always starts a new one)
- Browsing active runs and toggling items should be interactive, not command-driven
- The current CLI is optimized for LLM agents, not humans at a terminal

## What needs to be done

### Inline TUI for checklist runs
Build a compact, inline Bubbletea program (NOT full-screen/alt-screen) for working with checklist runs. Follow the pattern used by existing inline TUIs like the task picker (task_picker_helper.go) and log multiline editor (log.go) — these use tea.NewProgram() without WithAltScreen().

**Launch via 'rk cl run <template>'** — this is the primary entry point. If the template has an active run, resume it. If not, start a new run and enter the TUI.

- Show checklist items with checkboxes inline in the terminal
- Arrow keys (j/k) to navigate items, space/enter to toggle check
- Auto-complete detection: when all items checked, show completion message and exit
- 'a' to abandon the current run (mark abandoned, exit — does NOT start a new one)
- 'q' / Esc to quit the TUI while keeping the run active for later
- Compact rendering: just the checklist name, items with checkboxes, and a hint line for keybindings

### Service layer: add AbandonRun method
- Add AbandonRun(nameOrID) to checklist.Service that marks a run as abandoned WITHOUT starting a new one
- ResetRun should continue to work as-is (abandon + start fresh)
- This gives both TUI and CLI a clean abandon path

### CLI shortcut
- Add 'rk cl abandon <template>' as a CLI command for non-TUI abandon

## Design notes
- Inline TUI, not full-screen — same pattern as task_picker_helper.go and log.go
- The TUI is a standalone Bubbletea model in internal/cli/ (not internal/tui/) since it follows the same pattern as other CLI inline TUIs
- Keep it simple: one screen, one checklist at a time
- 'rk cl start' remains for non-interactive use (LLM agents); 'rk cl run' is the human-friendly entry point"}
old_value:
{"id":"reckon-yk1i","title":"Interactive TUI for checklist runs","description":"The checklist CLI commands are functional but high-friction for human use. Checking off items requires typing 'rk cl check <pos> --template=<name>' repeatedly. There is also no way to abandon a checklist run without immediately starting a new one (reset = abandon + start).

## Problem
- Checking items requires long CLI commands with --template flag each time
- No way to simply abandon a run (reset always starts a new one)
- Browsing active runs and toggling items should be interactive, not command-driven
- The current CLI is optimized for LLM agents, not humans at a terminal

## What needs to be done

### TUI for checklist interaction
Build a Bubbletea-based interactive view for working with checklist runs:
- Launch with 'rk checklist tui' or 'rk cl tui' (or consider 'rk cl run <template>' launching directly into TUI)
- Show active run(s) with checkbox items
- Arrow keys to navigate items, space/enter to toggle check
- Auto-complete detection (all items checked → congratulate + exit or prompt)
- 'a' or similar key to abandon the current run without starting a new one
- 'q' to quit the TUI (run stays active for later)
- If no active run, show template picker to start one

### Service layer: add AbandonRun method
- Add AbandonRun(nameOrID) to checklist.Service that marks a run as abandoned WITHOUT starting a new one
- ResetRun should continue to work as-is (abandon + start fresh)
- This gives both TUI and CLI a clean abandon path

### CLI shortcut (optional but nice)
- Add 'rk cl abandon <template>' as a CLI command for non-TUI abandon

## Design notes
- Follow existing TUI patterns in internal/tui/ (Bubbletea model, keyboard handling)
- The checklist TUI can be a standalone Bubbletea program (does not need to integrate into the main task TUI)
- Keep it simple: one screen, one checklist at a time
- Consider whether the TUI should also support starting runs and picking templates, or just operating on active runs","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"michael.biancalana@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-29T23:47:44Z","created_by":"Mike Biancalana","updated_at":"2026-03-29T23:47:44Z"}

{"id":"reckon-4u1c","title":"Add description field to task creation TUI form","description":"The inline TUI form for task creation (launched by 'rk task new' with no args) has fields for Title, Tags, Schedule, and Deadline — but no Description. The task file format already has a '## Description' section, but it just echoes the title text. There is no real description field on the Task model or in the service layer.

## Problem
- Tasks have no way to capture a description/body beyond the title
- The '## Description' section in the markdown file just duplicates the title (task_writer.go:51)
- The parser doesn't read a description back from the file
- The Task struct has no Description field

## What needs to be done

### Model: add Description field to Task
- Add Description string field to the Task struct in models.go
- Update NewTask to accept or default description

### Storage: wire description through read/write
- Update task_writer.go to write the actual description (not title) into '## Description'
- Update task_parser.go to parse the '## Description' section into the Description field
- Update task_service.go AddTask to accept a description parameter
- Update database storage if tasks are also stored in SQLite

### TUI form: add Description field
- Add a Description field to the task creation form in launchTaskNewForm()
- Use the TextEditor component (same as log.go) or a multiline form field type
- Description should be optional — title-only tasks are still fine
- Pass description through to createTaskFromForm → AddTask

### CLI flag
- Add --description flag to 'rk task new' for non-interactive use

## Design notes
- The form field order should be: Title, Description, Tags, Schedule, Deadline
- Description can be a simple multiline text field or a single-line field (match what the Form component supports)
- Backward compatible: existing tasks without descriptions should parse fine (empty description)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"michael.biancalana@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-29T23:58:53Z","created_by":"Mike Biancalana","updated_at":"2026-03-29T23:58:53Z"}
